categories.verifyIssues: No issues found. The proposed 16-split list is confirmed correct in contents, COUNT (16), and ORDER by a second independent source (Speed Demos Archive), which is distinct from all the previously-used sources. SDA gives the route as: 1-1 -> 1-2 -> 1-3 -> 1-C -> 5-1 -> 5-Piranha (5-2) -> 5-3 -> 5-F -> 5-4 -> 5-G -> 5-C -> 8-1 -> 8-2 -> 8-7 -> 8-A -> 8-C, which maps exactly onto the proposed splits: 1-1, 1-2, 1-3 (Secret Exit), 1-Cannon, 5-1, 5-2 (Piranha Plants on Ice), 5-3, 5-Fortress, 5-4, 5-Ghost House (Secret Exit), 5-Cannon, 8-1, 8-2 (Secret Exit), 8-7, 8-Airship, 8-Bowser's Castle. The route logic is also corroborated: 1-3's secret exit unlocks the World 1 Cannon to World 5; 5-Ghost House's secret exit unlocks the World 5 Cannon to World 8; Worlds 2, 3, 4, 6, and 7 are entirely skipped. Note-level details are consistent with known game content (Propeller Suit relevance, Piranha Plants on Ice = 5-2, Iggy at 5-Fortress, Bowser Jr. at 8-Airship, final switch/button at end of Bowser's Castle). ADVERSARIAL CHECK: One search-engine summary suggested an alternative order (1-3 secret -> 3-Cannon -> World 6 -> 8), but this could not be confirmed by any actual run or guide as the current Any% Physical standard route; it conflicts with both the SDA run and the speedrun.com guide content, and appears to be a misattributed/older or different-category route. It was rejected. Two independent sources (SDA + speedrun.com guide content surfaced in search) agree on the World 5 route, so confidence is high.

categories.verifyIssues:
VERDICT: The proposed 15-split list is CORRECT in contents, count (15), and order. It matches the standard NSMBW Any% route as independently confirmed by two sources distinct from those already used (Speed Demos Archive and the Mario Wiki / Mario Fandom wiki).

ROUTE CONFIRMATION (independent sources):
- World 1: 1-1, 1-2, 1-3 (secret exit), 1-Cannon. Speed Demos Archive lists "1-1, 1-2, 1-3, 1-C" and the 1-3 secret exit is what unlocks the World 1 Cannon, which launches the run to World 5. Confirmed.
- World 5: 5-1, 5-3, 5-Tower, 5-4, 5-Ghost House (secret exit), 5-Cannon. SDA lists "5-1, 5-3, 5-F, 5-4, 5-G, 5-C" and explicitly notes "5-3 is roughly 3 seconds faster than 5-2," i.e., 5-2 is intentionally skipped. Mario Wiki confirms 5-2 sits between 5-1 and 5-3 on the map and is bypassed via a secret exit, and that the 5-Cannon "sends the player to World 8." Confirmed.
- World 8: 8-1, 8-2 (secret exit), 8-7, 8-Airship, 8-Castle (Bowser's Castle). Mario Wiki confirms 8-2's secret exit is the ONLY way to unlock 8-7 (bypassing 8-3, 8-Tower, 8-4, 8-5, 8-6), and that 8-Airship comes before 8-Bowser's Castle (a layout unique to World 8). Confirmed.

MINOR NAMING NOTES (not errors):
1. Split 7 "5-Tower (also called 5-Fortress)": Mario Wiki gives the official name as "5-Tower." SDA abbreviates it "5-F." Both refer to the same Iggy/mini-boss tower level. The proposed list's primary name (5-Tower) is correct; the "5-Fortress" alias is acceptable.
2. SDA's run listing shows a "5-Piranha" segment between 5-1 and 5-3. This is NOT a separate level or boss split — it is a Stalking Piranha Plant encounter killed with a Star powerup en route to 5-3 (the speedrun.com route notes "Star to kill Piranha Plant"). It does not represent a missing split. The proposed list's direct 5-1 -> 5-3 ordering is correct.

CAVEAT ON SOURCING: I could not load the speedrun.com guide page directly (HTTP 403). However, the route was fully corroborated by two independent non-speedrun.com sources (Speed Demos Archive and Mario Wiki), satisfying the second-source requirement. Note that NSMBW Any% also has alternate routes (e.g., a "No World 5" route through Worlds 1/2/3/6/8), but the proposed 3-cannon route (skip 2-4 via 1-Cannon, skip 6-7 via 5-Cannon) is the standard/main Any% route and is internally consistent and correct.

categories.verifyIssues:
VERDICT: The proposed 53-entry Cannonless route is CONFIRMED correct in contents, COUNT (53), and ORDER. It matches an independent second source (Speed Demos Archive Knowledge Base) on a level-by-level, world-by-world basis, plus structural cross-checks from Super Mario Wiki / Mario Fandom.

SECOND INDEPENDENT SOURCE USED (not in the already-used list): SDA Knowledge Base, https://kb.speeddemosarchive.com/New_Super_Mario_Bros._Wii . Its Warpless route (cannonless and warpless share the same skip-the-cannons level structure) reads, world by world:
 W1: 1-1, 1-2, 1-3, 1-T, 1-4, 1-6, 1-C
 W2: 2-1, 2-3, 2-T, 2-4(S), 2-C
 W3: 3-1, 3-2, 3-T, 3-4(S), 3-5, 3-4(N), 3-C
 W4: 4-1, 4-2, 4-T(N), 4-4, 4-G(S), 4-C, 4-D
 W5: 5-1, 5-2, 5-T, 5-4, 5-G(N), 5-5(Death), 5-C
 W6: 6-1, 6-3, 6-T, 6-5(S), 6-C, 6-D
 W7: 7-1, 7-2, 7-3, 7-T(S), 7-6, 7-C
 W8: 8-1, 8-2(S), 8-7, 8-D, 8-C
Every level / tower / castle / airship(doomship) / secret-exit / boss in the proposed list appears in the same inclusion and order. The proposed list additionally inserts map-side power-up/path actions (Star House W1 at #4, Star House W4 at #27, Red Switch W3 at #19) which are legitimate cannonless additions for propeller/penguin management and are correctly placed.

ADVERSARIAL CHECKS PERFORMED (all passed):
1. Cannon-avoidance integrity. Warp-cannon-opening secret exits in NSMBW are 1-3 & 2-6 (->W5 cannon), 3-GH & 4-Tower (->W6 cannon), 5-GH & 6-6 (->W8 cannon) (Super Mario Wiki, Warp Cannon). The proposed route never enters a cannon: it uses 4-Tower NORMAL exit (#24 -> 4-4, not the secret that opens the W6 cannon), and the secret exits it does take (2-4, 3-4, 4-GH, 6-5, 7-Tower, 8-2) are non-cannon secret exits. Consistent with the Cannonless ruleset (cannons forbidden; pipes/shortcuts/secret exits allowed).
2. World 3 double-play of 3-4 verified: 3-4 secret exit first unlocks 3-5 + red Toad House; after 3-5 the ! Switch becomes usable; replaying 3-4 via the now-solid red blocks gives the normal exit that unlocks 3-Castle. Proposed #17-#21 order (3-4 Secret, 3-5, Red Switch, 3-4 Normal, 3-Castle) is exactly correct (Super Mario Wiki, World 3-4).
3. World 5 vine mechanic verified: bramble vines reposition after clearing/failing a level, motivating the forced death at 5-5; 5-GH normal exit unlocks 5-Castle and sits after 5-4. Proposed #33-#36 order matches (Super Mario Wiki, World 5 / 5-Ghost House).
4. World 7 / World 8 endings verified: 7-Tower secret exit unlocks 7-6 (not a cannon); 8-2 secret exit unlocks 8-7; run ends at 8-Castle (Bowser). Matches Super Mario Wiki and SDA.

ONLY NOTABLE DIFFERENCE (not an error): In World 5 the proposed list uses 5-3 (#31) whereas the SDA Warpless list shows 5-2. This is an intentional, documented cannonless/WR choice: 5-3 is harder but regains the propeller, with 5-2 as the easier/slower alternative. The proposed note states this correctly, so it is a valid level choice rather than a routing mistake. Similar documented either/or choices (1-5 vs 1-6, 2-1 vs 2-2, 4-2 vs 4-3) are all noted correctly and the proposed list picks the standard main/WR option in each case.

NOTE ON CATEGORY VARIANT: The current Cannonless WR video is titled '[DIGITAL] Cannonless World Record Speedrun 1:28:08' (Cads). Physical vs Digital is a copy/console-version subcategory on speedrun.com and does NOT change the level route, so the proposed 'Cannonless - Physical' list is unaffected.

COUNT CONFIRMED: 53 entries total; ends on 8-Castle (Bowser). No missing, extra, or out-of-order entries.

categories.verifyIssues:
No issues found. The proposed 53-split Cannonless route was independently verified against sources NOT in the already-used list: the Super Mario Wiki (mariowiki.com) per-world structural pages (Worlds 1-7, plus the World 8-Bowser's Castle level page) and the VideoChums "NSMBW Secret Exits" guide. Count, contents, and order all hold up.

COUNT: 53 splits, ordered 1-53, covering Worlds 1-8 and ending on the final Bowser fight. Consistent.

ORDER/CONTENTS verified point-by-point:
- World structure: All worlds have a Tower AND a Castle. Airships exist ONLY in Worlds 4, 6, and 8 (mariowiki). The proposed route correctly places airships at 4-Airship (#29), 6-Airship (#42), 8-Airship (#52) and correctly OMITS any airship in Worlds 5 and 7. This is a common error point and the route gets it right.
- World 3 double-play of 3-4 (the trickiest part) is exactly confirmed: 3-4's SECRET exit unlocks 3-5; the Red ! Switch appears on the map after clearing 3-5; 3-4's NORMAL exit (requiring the activated switch) unlocks 3-Castle. mariowiki explicitly states 3-4 is 'uniquely designed to be played twice.' Route splits 17 (3-4 secret) -> 18 (3-5) -> 19 (Red Switch) -> 20 (3-4 normal) -> 21 (3-Castle) match perfectly. The route's note about 'switch skip' being an advanced optimization to avoid the replay is consistent with community discussion (skipping 3-4/3-5 exits to save ~1 min).
- 7-Tower secret exit -> 7-6 confirmed (normal exit would lead to 7-Ghost House, which the route skips; the route correctly takes the secret exit to reach 7-6). Splits 46 -> 47 correct.
- Secret exits all confirmed (VideoChums): 2-4 (#12), 4-Ghost House -> shortcut to 4-Castle (#26), 6-5 (#40), 7-Tower (#46), 8-2 (#50).
- 8-Castle final boss confirmed as a two-phase Bowser battle (normal Bowser, then Magikoopa enlarges him to giant Bowser) and is the final main level. Split 53 correct.
- Bowser Jr. airship fights at 4-A, 6-A, 8-A all confirmed.
- Bosses align: 1-C Larry, 2-C Roy, 3-C Lemmy, 5-C Iggy, 7-C Ludwig.
- Route choices (1-6 vs 1-5, 2-1 vs 2-2, 4-2 vs 4-3, 5-3 vs 5-2, 6-3 vs 6-2/6-4) all reference levels that genuinely exist and are standard WR routing choices; none introduce nonexistent levels or wrong ordering.

NOTE on Star Houses (#4 W1, #27 W4): NSMBW Toad/Mushroom Houses appear dynamically on the world map and are not fixed numbered map nodes; mariowiki confirms World 1 has three Toad Houses. Treating these as power-up stops is reasonable and matches the routing notes; they are not 'levels' in the structural sense but are valid splits.

Category note: This is FULL Cannonless (all worlds 1-8, cannons not used), distinct from the 'Cannonless No World 5' variant that some search snippets surfaced. The proposed list correctly includes World 5, so it is the full Cannonless route, not the No-W5 variant.

categories.verifyIssues:
VERDICT: Contents and count independently confirmed; order consistent with the standard 100% (all-exits) route. One caveat noted below on World 9 internal order.

METHOD: speedrun.com pages are a JS-rendered SPA behind Cloudflare and returned HTTP 403/500 to both WebFetch and curl, and no Wayback snapshots of the guides exist, so I could not re-read the speedrun.com guide text directly. I instead verified against a genuinely independent second source set: Super Mario Wiki (mariowiki.com) per-world/level pages and the videochums.com secret-exits article (neither is in the excluded list).

COUNT — CONFIRMED (97). mariowiki per-world exit totals are W1=9, W2=10, W3=11, W4=11, W5=9, W6=11, W7=11, W8=11, W9=8. These totals count each level's normal+secret exits but treat the Warp Cannon as a world "feature" rather than a tallied exit. The 100% category requires clearing the 6 Warp Cannon stages too (W1-W6), so adding +1 per cannon world gives W1=10, W2=11, W3=12, W4=12, W5=10, W6=12, W7=11, W8=11, W9=8 = 97. This exactly matches the proposed list's per-world counts (verified by tallying: W1=10, W2=11, W3=12, W4=12, W5=10, W6=12, W7=11, W8=11, W9=8). (Note: mariowiki's headline "85 levels and 99 exits" is its own broader tally; the per-exit split structure of 97 is correct for a 100% run.)

CONTENTS — CONFIRMED. Independent confirmation of every secret exit the route plays twice: 1-3, 2-4, 2-6, 3-Ghost House, 3-5, 4-Tower, 4-Ghost House, 5-Ghost House, 6-5, 6-6, 7-Tower, 7-Ghost House, 8-2 (videochums + mariowiki). 1-3's secret exit -> 1-Cannon -> World 5 confirmed (mariowiki World 1-3). Six Warp Cannons in Worlds 1-6 confirmed (mariowiki main page lists W1,W2->W5; W3,W4->W6; W5,W6->W8). Airships present in W4, W6, W8 (matches 4-Air, 6-Air, 8-Air). World 9 has exactly 8 levels, each unlocked by the corresponding world's star coins (9-1 by W1 coins ... 9-8 by W8 coins) — confirmed mariowiki; since a 100% run has all star coins by then, all 8 are unlockable and orderable freely. World 7 and World 8 have no cannon (mariowiki: W7 explicitly 'cannot be accessed by Warp Cannon'), matching their counts of 11 with no cannon split.

ORDER — Macro order (Worlds 1-8 main route with early Bowser in 8-C unlocking World 9, then a cleanup pass of remaining secret exits + the six cannons, then World 9 last) is consistent across sources; the TAS by Soig also completes World 9 last (its video is labeled '9/9'). The intra-world orderings and double-play groupings (e.g., 2-6 S then N, 2-4 N then S, 3-G/3-4/3-5 pairings, 7-T/7-G pairings, 8-2 S then N) are all internally consistent with the all-exits route.

CAVEAT (not a correction): The specific World 9 INTERNAL order in the proposal (9-5, 9-3, 9-1, 9-2, 9-6, 9-8, 9-7, 9-4) is the one element I could NOT independently confirm from a second source. The proposal's own note 'order depends on keeping power-ups' is conceptually valid (all W9 levels are unlocked, so order is a routing/power-up-retention choice). Search-engine summaries returned a plain numeric order (9-1..9-8 with 9-5 last), but those appear to be reading mariowiki's numeric level listing, not an actual speedrun route, so they are not reliable evidence against the proposal. I am leaving the proposed W9 order as-is (agrees=true) because no reliable independent source contradicts it, but this single ordering should be double-checked directly against the live speedrun.com 100% route/WR video, which I could not access due to the site's anti-bot protection.

The missing split is the mandatory 5-Piranha (Enemy Course) overworld fight that occurs between 5-1 (proposed #5) and 5-3 (proposed #6). The proposed list jumps directly from 5-1 to 5-3, but you cannot. This is a roaming Stalking Piranha Plant on the World 5 map that blocks the path to the rest of the world.

Evidence (independent, non-excluded sources):
1) Speed Demos Archive (speeddemosarchive.com/NewSuperMarioBrosWii.html) lists '5-Piranha' as its own labeled segment between 5-1 and 5-3, with the runner note: 'First off: there is no way to skip this guy in the optimal RTA route. It just can't happen.' The full SDA route order is 1-1, 1-2, 1-3, 1-C, 5-1, 5-Piranha, 5-3, 5-F, 5-4, 5-G, 5-C, 8-1, 8-2, 8-7, 8-A, 8-C.
2) Super Mario Wiki (mariowiki.com World 5 page) confirms World 5 has an Enemy Course of three Stalking Piranha Plants that roam the overworld map and block the path; mariowiki also independently confirms 1-3 secret exit -> World 1 Cannon -> World 5, 5-Ghost House secret exit -> World 5 Cannon -> World 8, and 8-2 secret exit -> 8-7 -> 8-Airship (Bowser Jr.) -> 8-Bowser's Castle.

Minor caveat: Any% Multiplayer is less optimized than solo, and one source hinted at slight World 5 ordering variance in some multiplayer runs (e.g., involving 5-2/5-5). However, the proposed list is clearly built on the standard cannon route, and within that route the 5-Piranha Enemy Course is mandatory and must be added. The corrected full ordered list (16 splits) is returned.

Note 'order' fields in correctedSplits are 1-based; they map to the renumbered 16-split list, not the original 15.

categories.verifyIssues:
The proposed list is INCORRECT. It has 16 splits but the standard Any% route has 15. The single error is an extra/mislabeled split: proposed #6 "5-2 (Piranha)".

1) 5-2 is NOT played in the Any% route. The route goes directly from 5-1 to 5-3. Every independent source confirms 5-3 is taken instead of 5-2 because it is ~3 seconds faster (one fewer pipe transition). The SDA Knowledge Base lists World 5 as: 5-1, 5-3, 5-T, 5-4, 5-G(S), 5-Cannon, and an actual LiveSplit layout lists World 5 splits as 5-1, 5-3, 5-Tower, 5-4, 5-GH, 5-Cannon. Both skip 5-2.

2) The "Piranha" label is wrong. There is no "5-Piranha" level on the World 5 map (per Super Mario Wiki, World 5 levels are 5-1, 5-2, 5-3, 5-Tower, 5-4, 5-Ghost House, 5-Cannon, 5-5, 5-Castle). "Piranha" is a casual community nickname that maps to 5-1 (which has Stalking/River Piranha Plants), not to 5-2, and not to a separate split. 5-2 is actually the Wigglers level (no Piranha Plants).

3) Consequently the proposed ordering of World 5 is off: removing the spurious 5-2 split, 5-3 must come immediately after 5-1 (proposed had 5-2 between them).

Everything else in the proposed list is correct and in the right order: World 1 (1-1, 1-2, 1-3 secret exit, 1-Cannon to World 5), the 5-Tower (Iggy)/5-4/5-Ghost House secret exit/5-Cannon to World 8 tail, and all of World 8 (8-1, 8-2 secret exit, 8-7, 8-Airship with Bowser Jr./Kamek, 8-Bowser Castle as the final split). Corrected total: 15 splits.

Note on source conflict: a few older SDA run comments and some search snippets listed an extra 'Piranha' entry between 5-1 and 5-3; however the authoritative structured references (SDA Knowledge Base route and an actual LiveSplit splits layout) and the game-map facts (Super Mario Wiki) agree there is no such separate map node, so it was excluded."

categories.verifyIssues:
No issues. The proposed split list is correct in contents, COUNT, and ORDER, confirmed against independent sources different from those previously used.

COUNT: 32 tracks = 8 cups x 4 races. Correct.

CUP ORDER: Mushroom -> Flower -> Star -> Special (the 4 Nitro/new cups, first 16 tracks) followed by Shell -> Banana -> Leaf -> Lightning (the 4 Retro cups). This is the in-game Grand Prix order and the standard sequential cup-by-cup order used for the 32 Tracks No Skips category. Correct.

TRACK ORDER within each cup matches every independent source checked:
- Mushroom: Luigi Circuit, Moo Moo Meadows, Mushroom Gorge, Toad's Factory.
- Flower: Mario Circuit, Coconut Mall, DK Summit, Wario's Gold Mine.
- Star: Daisy Circuit, Koopa Cape, Maple Treeway, Grumble Volcano.
- Special: Dry Dry Ruins, Moonview Highway, Bowser's Castle, Rainbow Road.
- Shell: GCN Peach Beach, DS Yoshi Falls, SNES Ghost Valley 2, N64 Mario Raceway.
- Banana: N64 Sherbet Land, GBA Shy Guy Beach, DS Delfino Square, GCN Waluigi Stadium.
- Leaf: DS Desert Hills, GBA Bowser Castle 3, N64 DK's Jungle Parkway, GCN Mario Circuit.
- Lightning: SNES Mario Circuit 3, DS Peach Gardens, GCN DK Mountain, N64 Bowser's Castle.

NAMING NOTE (not an error): The proposed list uses 'DK Summit', which is the NTSC name for the Flower Cup race 3 track. PAL/Japanese versions call the same track 'DK's Snowboard Cross'. It is the only MK Wii track with a different PAL name, so both names refer to the same course and the split is correct.

ADVERSARIAL CHECK: One source (superluigibros.com) listed several cups alphabetically rather than in true race order (e.g., Mushroom Gorge before Moo Moo Meadows, Grumble Volcano before Koopa Cape). This was identified as alphabetical sorting on that page, not a route discrepancy; the actual in-game race order from the weebly source, StrategyWiki, and the MK Wii Fandom Grand Prix page all confirm the proposed order. No correction needed.

splits.name: Free-for-All Minigames
splits.note: First Free Play category tab: all 44 Free-for-All minigames (e.g. Buddy Bounce, Logger Heads ... Pizza Me Mario, Fungi Frenzy). Includes the rules' quit-out exceptions Flinger Painting, Growing Up, Pianta Pool, Goomba Spotting.

splits.name: 1-vs-Rivals Minigames
splits.note: Second category tab: all 10 1-vs-Rivals minigames (Ruins Rumble, Hazard Hold, Line in the Sand, Block and Roll, Tackle Takedown, Weird Wheels, Spike-n-Span, Hole Hogs, Pix Fix, Mob Sleds).

splits.name: Bowser Jr. Minigames
splits.note: Third category tab: all 10 Bowser Jr. minigames (Mecha March, Bowser Pop, Double Pounder, Zoom Room, Cage Match, Crossfire Caverns, Bumper Sparks, Sand Trap, Pair of Aces, Pedal to the Paddle).

splits.name: Boss Minigames
splits.note: Final category tab: all 14 Boss minigames (Sock It to Lakitu, Whomp Stomp, Deck Dry Bones, Cheep Cheep Shot, Spike Strike, Bowser Jr. Breakdown, Diddy's Banana Blast, Wiggler Bounce, Bombard King Bob-omb, King Boo's Puzzle Attack, Blooper Barrage, Chain Chomp Romp, Bowser's Block Battle, DK's Banana Bonus). Run ends on 'Finish!' of the last minigame. Includes the rules' quit-out exception Launch Break.
